title: Community Spaces Polling January 2026
short_description: YouGov carried out polling on behalf of the GLA about Londoners' use of community spaces. Where YouGov Plc collects survey data, no information may be...
long_description: YouGov carried out polling on behalf of the GLA about Londoners' use of community spaces. Where YouGov Plc collects survey data, no information may be published without the consent of YouGov Plc and the GLA. All figures, unless otherwise stated, are from YouGov Plc on behalf of the GLA. The surveys were carried out online. The figures have been weighted and are representative of all London adults (aged 18+). All results are based on a sample and are therefore subject to statistical errors normally associated with sample-based information.

title: Census Commissioned Tables 2001, 2011 and 2021
short_description: Until recently it was difficult to find out what commissioned tables were available from these censuses. ONS hold copies of all the tables published from...
long_description: Until recently it was difficult to find out what commissioned tables were available from these censuses. ONS hold copies of all the tables published from the 2021, 2011 and 2001 censuses but they didn't publish details of these. In October 2024 they released lists of the tables available from 2001 to 2021 and since then have updated the lists periodically. ONS lists of commissioned tables 2001,2011 and 2021 Lists for 2011 and 2021 give hyperlinks to the data. All the 2021 and 2011 tables are now on the ONS website, but some of the earlier 2011 tables haven't always been. Hence some earlier 2011 tables are hyperlinked to archived versions. title: Census Commissioned Tables 2001 data files
short_description: Original data files for 2001 Census Commissioned Tables listed in this file: list of 2001 commissioned tables Most were produced in excel xls format, some...
long_description: Original data files for 2001 Census Commissioned Tables listed in this file: list of 2001 commissioned tables Most were produced in excel xls format, some of the larger files are csv or xlsx. Many of the larger commissioned tables were produced in multiple parts with each part a separate file. For 2001 there were three series of commissioned outputs starting with "M", "ES", and the usual "C". Data files here are presented in zip folders: All the M tables have been included in a single (rather large) folder, with the ES tables included in a single much smaller folder; C tables make up most of the output. title: London Privacy Register
short_description: This dataset is a central catalogue of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) of smart city projects that collect personal information in public spaces....
long_description: This dataset is a central catalogue of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) of smart city projects that collect personal information in public spaces. By publishing this in one place for the first time, it will enable public transparency and support good practice among operators. A DPIA helps to identify and minimise the risks of a project that uses personal data. Further information: DPIA registration form: https://www.london.gov.uk/dpia-register-form Information Commissioner DPIA: https://ico.org.uk/for-organisations/guide-to-data-protection/guide-to-the-general-data-protection-regulation-gdpr/accountability-and-governance/data-protection-impact-assessments/

title: GLA population projection service
short_description: The GLA Demography Team offers a bespoke population projection service to London local authorities. Boroughs can request population projections based on...
long_description: The GLA Demography Team offers a bespoke population projection service to London local authorities. Boroughs can request population projections based on their own choice of assumptions about future housing delivery. These assumptions are submitted to the team via a standard template. The resulting projections are referred to as the Borough Preferred Option (or BPO) and are commonly used to help support local planning and service delivery. The GLA does not make the BPO projections and submitted housing trajectories publicly available or share them with anyone other than the commissioning borough. title: Community Life Survey - London summary
short_description: The Community Life survey is a household self-completion survey of adults aged 16+ in England. The survey provides evidence on social cohesion, community...
long_description: The Community Life survey is a household self-completion survey of adults aged 16+ in England. The survey provides evidence on social cohesion, community engagement and social action. This report summarises the results from the survey and provides comparisons between London and the rest of England. There are other surveys that provide more granular evidence on these topics within London, but this survey has been selected to provide a regional comparison. Further details of the breakdowns presented in this report can be accessed through the data tables on the Community Life survey webpage .
